title: Common token-validation pitfalls
description: alg:none, algorithm confusion, unverified claims, missing aud/iss checks, and clock-skew handling are recurring JWT bugs.
body: Use when consuming or debugging a signed token. Recurring defects: accepting alg:none; algorithm confusion (RS256 public key used as HS256 secret); decoding claims without verifying the signature; skipping issuer/audience checks; ignoring expiry or unbounded clock skew; trusting a role/scope claim the issuer never set. Do not read any claim before signature verification. Actions: pin the expected algorithm, verify with the right key, check iss/aud/exp/nbf with bounded skew, then read claims. Validation signals include tests for tampered-signature, alg:none, wrong-issuer, and expired tokens all rejected. Risk boundary: trusting an unverified role/scope claim is a critical finding.
